engineIntelligence.h2: Seat Ibiza Engines- Codes, Common Failures& Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Seat Ibiza in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All prices reflect supply-only ranges from our specialist network.

cards.altText: Seat Ibiza CBZB engine- petrol 1.2L replacement UK
productionHistory: Ibiza IV (6J/6P) Reference, SE, Style, FR- 2009-2017
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure- premature wear causes rattling noise and potential engine damage from 60k miles.
Oil consumption increase- piston ring wear on high-mileage units leads to excessive oil use.

cards.altText: Seat Ibiza CAYC engine- diesel 1.6L replacement UK
productionHistory: Ibiza IV (6J/6P) Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR- 2009-2017
knownFailures: Injector failure- high-pressure injectors degrade causing rough running and misfires after 80k miles.
EGR cooler cracking- known fault leading to coolant loss and potential head gasket damage.

cards.altText: Seat Ibiza DKRF engine- petrol 1.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Ibiza V (KJ1) Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR- 2017-present
knownFailures: Intake manifold carbon buildup- causes hesitation and reduced performance on direct-injection units.
High-pressure fuel pump wear- may cause hard starting or reduced power on higher-mileage examples.

engineGuide.h2: Seat Ibiza Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications& Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Seat Ibiza across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The CAYC 1.6 TDI powered most diesel Ibiza IV models, offering strong economy but becoming notorious for injector and EGR cooler failures at higher mileage.
commonFailures: High-pressure injector wear causing rough running and misfires from 80k miles
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss and potential head gasket damage
Dual-mass flywheel wear on manual transmission models causing rattling noise
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Diesel
entries.history: The smaller-displacement CAYB 1.2 TDI offered an entry-level diesel option for early Ibiza IV models with lower power but excellent fuel economy.
commonFailures: Similar injector and EGR patterns to CAYC but less documented due to lower volume
Turbo actuator wear on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAYB Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced for the Ibiza IV facelift, the BMS 1.4 TDI balanced improved emissions compliance with usable torque for urban driving.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey cars leading to clogging
EGR valve sticking causing reduced power and limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for BMS Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The CBZB 1.2 TSI was the most common petrol engine in Ibiza IV, but its EA111 timing chain tensioner design flaw made it the single biggest driver of replacement enquiries.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling noise and potential valve damage from 60k miles
Piston ring wear leading to excessive oil consumption on early-production units
High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZB Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The CBZC is a variant of the CBZB 1.2 TSI with minor calibration differences, sharing the same timing chain vulnerability and replacement considerations.
commonFailures: Identical timing chain tensioner failure pattern to CBZB
Oil consumption issues on early-production units
entries.cta: Get quotes for CBZC Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The CZEA 1.4 TSI powered Ibiza IV FR and Cupra models, delivering strong performance but requiring careful maintenance due to turbocharged direct-injection complexity.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner issues on early EA111 1.4 TSI units
Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring periodic walnut blasting
Turbo actuator wear causing boost pressure faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for CZEA Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The CZDA is a closely related variant of the CZEA 1.4 TSI with minor output differences, sharing the same performance characteristics and failure patterns.
commonFailures: Same timing chain and carbon buildup concerns as CZEA
High-pressure fuel pump wear on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CZDA Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The DKRF 1.0 TSI replaced the 1.2 TSI for Ibiza V, offering improved refinement and efficiency with three-cylinder design- though direct injection requires intake maintenance.
commonFailures: Intake manifold carbon buildup causing hesitation and misfires on direct-injection units
High-pressure fuel pump wear leading to hard starting or reduced power
Timing chain stretch on very high-mileage examples(less common than EA111)
entries.cta: Get quotes for DKRF Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The DKLA is the lower-output variant of the 1.0 TSI family, sharing the same EA211 architecture and maintenance requirements as the DKRF.
commonFailures: Intake carbon buildup pattern identical to DKRF
HPFP wear on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DKLA Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The CHZJ is another variant within the EA211 1.0 TSI family, with calibration differences but shared architecture and failure patterns.
commonFailures: Same intake manifold carbon concerns as other EA211 1.0 TSI codes
Occasional turbo actuator faults on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CHZJ Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: The DADA 1.5 TSI Evo arrived with the Ibiza V facelift as the new performance petrol option, featuring cylinder deactivation for improved efficiency without sacrificing power.
commonFailures: Early units may experience intake manifold flap motor faults
Carbon buildup remains a consideration for direct-injection design
Turbo-related issues less common but possible on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for DADA Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The CGPA 1.0 MPI provided a simple, naturally aspirated entry-level petrol option for early Ibiza V models, prioritising affordability over performance.
commonFailures: Generally robust with fewer catastrophic failure patterns than turbocharged units
Occasional ignition coil or sensor faults on higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for CGPA Seat Ibiza engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The BXW 1.4 MPI was a carry-over naturally aspirated engine for early Ibiza IV models, offering simple reliability but lower performance versus TSI options.
commonFailures: Generally reliable with minimal major failure patterns documented
Occasional thermostat or water pump leaks on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BXW Seat Ibiza engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Seat Ibiza Engine Problems- What It Costs to Fix& When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Seat Ibiza's most common replacement enquiries centre on the 1.2 TSI(CBZB/CBZC) timing chain, 1.6 TDI(CAYC) injector/EGR failures, and 1.0 TSI(DKRF) intake manifold issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point- plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.group: Seat Ibiza 1.2 TSI Timing Chain Failure- Why CBZB/CBZC Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.h4: Seat Ibiza 1.2 TSI Timing Chain Failure- Why CBZB/CBZC Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza Reference, SE, Style, FR(2009-2017, CBZB& CBZC 1.2 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that damages guides and can jump timing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head work if valves bent; new chain kit included.
repairOptions.longevity: Only if damage limited; often not cost-effective on older Ibizas.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt CBZB/CBZC with upgraded tensioner, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage cars; restores reliability long-term.

problems.recommendation: If your Ibiza is under 70k miles and showing early chain rattle, a preventative kit from a specialist offers the best value. Once the chain has jumped or valves are bent, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is often more economical than extensive repairs- especially on 10+ year old models.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CBZB engine prices for your Seat Ibiza

problems.group: Seat Ibiza 1.6 TDI Injector& EGR Failure- CAYC Diesel Replacement Guide
problems.h4: Seat Ibiza 1.6 TDI Injector& EGR Failure- CAYC Diesel Replacement Guide
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR(2009-2017, CAYC 1.6 TDI)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injectors wear causing poor atomisation; EGR cooler cracks from thermal stress leading to coolant loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test and refurbish injectors; EGR blanking if applicable.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may delay but not prevent eventual replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injectors and/or EGR cooler with OEM-spec parts.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; labour-intensive on transverse TDI.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt CAYC with upgraded injectors, EGR delete option, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution for high-mileage diesel Ibizas.

problems.recommendation: For Ibizas over 100k miles with multiple diesel faults, investing in a rebuilt CAYC engine often makes more financial sense than piecemeal repairs. The upgraded internals in a quality rebuild address the root causes of injector and EGR failure.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CAYC Seat Ibiza diesel engine replacement

problems.group: Seat Ibiza 1.0 TSI Intake Manifold Issues- DKRF/DKLA Carbon Buildup
problems.h4: Seat Ibiza 1.0 TSI Intake Manifold Issues- DKRF/DKLA Carbon Buildup
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR(2017-present, DKRF/DKLA 1.0 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design allows carbon deposits to accumulate on intake valves, restricting airflow and causing misfires.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold and blast valves clean; no parts replacement.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective maintenance; recommended every 60k miles for direct-injection TSIs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty intake manifold assembly with updated part number.
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses flap motor failures; does not prevent future carbon buildup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt 1.0 TSI with cleaned/updated manifold, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if engine has additional issues like turbo or HPFP wear.

problems.recommendation: Regular walnut blasting every 50-60k miles is the most cost-effective way to maintain a 1.0 TSI Ibiza. If the engine has suffered misfire-related damage or has high mileage alongside manifold issues, a rebuilt unit offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DKRF engine prices for your Seat Ibiza

problems.group: Seat Ibiza Oil Consumption- Early TSI Piston Ring Wear(CBZB/CBZC)
problems.h4: Seat Ibiza Oil Consumption- Early TSI Piston Ring Wear(CBZB/CBZC)
problems.affectedModels: Seat Ibiza Reference, SE, Style(2009-2015, CBZB/CBZC 1.2 TSI)
problems.rootCause: Early EA111 1.2 TSI engines had piston ring design that allowed excessive oil passage into combustion chamber.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Regular oil level checks and top-ups between services.
repairOptions.longevity: Only delays inevitable; not a repair, just damage limitation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine out, strip bottom end, fit updated piston rings and seals.
repairOptions.longevity: Labour-intensive; often approaches cost of rebuilt unit on older Ibizas.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply& fit rebuilt engine with updated piston design, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Most economical permanent solution; eliminates oil consumption issue entirely.

problems.recommendation: If your 1.2 TSI Ibiza is burning over 1 litre per 1,000 miles and is over 70k miles, a rebuilt engine with updated pistons is almost always better value than an in-situ ring replacement. The warranty coverage also provides peace of mind that top-ups cannot offer.
problems.cta: Find a reconditioned CBZB Seat Ibiza engine with warranty

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Seat Ibiza
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with timing chain rattle on a 1.2 TSI, injector failure on a 1.6 TDI, or carbon buildup on a 1.0 TSI, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Seat Ibiza specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Seat Ibiza Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Seat Ibiza and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty- always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Seat Ibiza Engines by Size- Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Seat Ibiza across UK generations is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Seat Ibiza 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TDI(BMS) was introduced for the Ibiza IV facelift(2014-2017) as an efficient entry-level diesel option, offering strong low-end torque for urban driving while meeting Euro 6 emissions standards.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging on short-journey cars; DPF regeneration issues if not driven regularly on motorways.
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat Ibiza 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Seat Ibiza 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 TDI(CAYC/CAYB) was the backbone of the Ibiza IV diesel range(2009-2017), offering an excellent balance of fuel economy and performance- but known for injector and EGR cooler failures at higher mileage.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure injector wear from 80k miles; EGR cooler cracking causing coolant loss; dual-mass flywheel wear on manual models.
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat Ibiza 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Seat Ibiza 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0 TSI(DKRF/DKLA/CHZJ) replaced the 1.2 TSI for Ibiza V(2017-present), offering improved efficiency and refinement with three-cylinder smoothness- though direct injection requires regular intake cleaning.
commonFailurePoints: Intake manifold carbon buildup on direct-injection units; high-pressure fuel pump wear on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Seat Ibiza 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Ibiza 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2 TSI(CBZB/CBZC) powered most Ibiza IV petrol models(2009-2017) and remains the most common replacement engine due to widespread timing chain tensioner failures on early EA111 units.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causing rattling
potential valve damage from 60k miles; piston ring wear leading to oil consumption.
items.cta: Compare Seat Ibiza 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Ibiza 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TSI(CZEA/CZDA) was reserved for Ibiza IV FR and Cupra models(2015-2017), delivering strong performance with turbocharged power- though complexity means higher replacement costs.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner issues on early EA111 1.4 TSI; carbon buildup on intake valves; turbo actuator wear.
items.cta: Compare Seat Ibiza 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Ibiza 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5 TSI Evo(DADA) arrived with the Ibiza V facelift(2018-present) as the new performance petrol option, featuring cylinder deactivation for improved efficiency without sacrificing power.
commonFailurePoints: Early units may experience intake manifold flap issues; carbon buildup remains a consideration for direct-injection design.
items.cta: Compare Seat Ibiza 1.5L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Seat Ibiza needs? Enter your registration number above- we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Seat Ibiza specialists.

fuelTypes.h2: Seat Ibiza Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Seat Ibiza engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options- from early 1.2 TSI to the latest 1.5 TSI Evo.

items.title: Seat Ibiza Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel Seat Ibiza models(primarily 1.4 TDI and 1.6 TDI) were popular for high-mileage drivers but face expensive injector and EGR failures that often drive replacement decisions on older IV-generation cars.
items.descriptor: Diesel Seat Ibiza models(primarily 1.4 TDI and 1.6 TDI) were popular for high-mileage drivers but face expensive injector and EGR failures that often drive replacement decisions on older IV-generation cars.
items.familiesLabel: Common Seat Ibiza Diesel Engine Families
families: CAYC- 1.6 TDI(75-105 PS), most common diesel replacement engine(Reference/SE/FR), injector&EGR failure risk
CAYB- 1.2 TDI(75 PS), entry-level diesel for early Ibiza IV, less common but similar failure patterns
BMS- 1.4 TDI(75-90 PS), facelift Ibiza IV diesel, improved emissions but still susceptible to DPF/EGR issues
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy(60+ mpg achievable)
Injector and EGR cooler failures common after 80k miles
High UK replacement demand on 1.6 TDI Reference and SE models
items.cta: Get quotes for Seat Ibiza Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Seat Ibiza Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Seat Ibiza engines(1.0 TSI, 1.2 TSI, 1.4/1.5 TSI) dominate the replacement market, with the 1.2 TSI timing chain issue being the single biggest driver of enquiries across the Ibiza IV generation.
items.descriptor: Petrol Seat Ibiza engines(1.0 TSI, 1.2 TSI, 1.4/1.5 TSI) dominate the replacement market, with the 1.2 TSI timing chain issue being the single biggest driver of enquiries across the Ibiza IV generation.
items.familiesLabel: Common Seat Ibiza Petrol Engine Families
families: CBZB/CBZC- 1.2 TSI(85-105 PS), highest-volume replacement engine, notorious timing chain tensioner failure
DKRF/DKLA- 1.0 TSI(95-115 PS), Ibiza V workhorse, intake carbon buildup requires maintenance
CZEA/DADA- 1.4/1.5 TSI(140-150 PS), FR/Cupra performance units, higher replacement cost but strong demand
foundIn: Ibiza IV(6J/6P)(2008-2017)- CBZB, CBZC, CZEA, CZDA
Ibiza V(KJ1)(2017-present)- DKRF, DKLA, CHZJ, DADA
knownFor: 1.2 TSI timing chain failures between 60k-90k miles drive most replacement enquiries
1.0 TSI direct injection requires regular walnut blasting to prevent carbon issues
FR and Cupra TSI engines command premium replacement prices but offer strong performance
typicalModels: Seat Ibiza FR 1.0 TSI / 1.2 TSI / 1.4 TSI / 1.5 TSI
items.cta: Compare Seat Ibiza Petrol engine prices

items.title: Seat Ibiza Hybrid/ Electrified Engines
items.description: The Seat Ibiza has not been offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all replacement enquiries relate to conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.descriptor: The Seat Ibiza has not been offered with hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market; all replacement enquiries relate to conventional petrol or diesel engines.
items.familiesLabel: Common Seat Ibiza Hybrid/ Electrified Engine Families
importantNotes: No mild-hybrid, plug-in hybrid or full-electric Ibiza variants were sold in the UK
All engine replacement options are for conventional combustion engines only
Future electrified variants would require specialist high-voltage trained suppliers
items.cta: Enquire about Seat Ibiza conventional engine availability

engineYears.h2: Seat Ibiza Engines by Year- What Was Fitted& When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Seat Ibiza production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The fourth-generation Ibiza launched with carry-over VW Group engines including the problematic early 1.2 TSI and new 1.6 TDI diesel.
keyChanges: New PQ25 platform debut for Ibiza IV(6J chassis)
Introduction of EA111 1.2 TSI(CBZB/CBZC) petrol engines
New EA189 1.6 TDI(CAYC) diesel replaces older Pumpe-Düse units
Reference, SE, Style trim levels at launch
mainEngines: CBZB- 1.2L petrol TSI(85-105 PS)(Reference, SE, Style)
CAYC- 1.6L diesel TDI(90-105 PS)(Reference, SE, Style)
knownFor: Early CBZB 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner issues emerge in owner reports
Strong diesel demand for high-mileage drivers
Value-focused positioning drives high used-engine replacement volume
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2009 Seat Ibiza replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Ibiza 1.2 TSI engine replacement • CBZB timing chain rattle • 1.6 TDI injector failure

years.description: Engine line-up stabilises with CBZB 1.2 TSI and CAYC 1.6 TDI dominating sales; timing chain concerns grow for early 1.2 TSI units.
keyChanges: Minor trim updates; engine codes unchanged
FR performance variant introduced with 1.4 TSI(CZEA)
Growing owner reports of 1.2 TSI timing chain noise
knownFor: CBZB timing chain tensioner failures become widespread from 60k miles
FR 1.4 TSI attracts performance enthusiasts but commands premium replacement costs
CAYC diesel remains reliable if serviced but injector failures emerge post-80k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2012 Seat Ibiza replacement engines

years.description: The 6P facelift brought updated styling, Euro 6-compliant engines and new 1.0 TSI code introductions alongside carry-over 1.2 TSI units.
keyChanges: Facelift styling and interior updates(6P chassis code)
Introduction of EA211 1.0 TSI(DKRF) for select markets
BMS 1.4 TDI added as new entry-level diesel option
XCELLENCE trim introduced as new top-spec diesel variant
knownFor: Timing chain issues persist on carry-over CBZB engines
New BMS 1.4 TDI shows improved emissions but DPF concerns on short-journey cars
DKRF 1.0 TSI preview hints at future EA211 dominance
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2014 Seat Ibiza replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Ibiza 6P engine codes • 1.4 TDI DPF problems • 1.2 TSI vs 1.0 TSI reliability

years.description: Final production years for Ibiza IV see Cupra performance variant launch and growing awareness of 1.2 TSI replacement needs.
keyChanges: Cupra flagship variant launched with 1.8 TSI(CJSB) and later 1.4 TSI(CZEA)
Final CBZB 1.2 TSI production; replacement demand peaks for 2009-2012 units
Growing specialist network for rebuilt EA111 engines
knownFor: CBZB replacement market matures with multiple rebuilt suppliers now offering upgraded tensioners
Cupra 1.4 TSI commands premium pricing but strong enthusiast demand
CAYC diesel remains popular for high-mileage replacements if injectors addressed
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2016 Seat Ibiza replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Ibiza Cupra engine replacement • CBZB rebuilt with upgraded tensioner • 1.6 TDI injector rebuild vs replace

years.description: All-new MQB A0 platform brings EA211 1.0 TSI(DKRF/DKLA) as the new mainstream engine, largely retiring the problematic 1.2 TSI.
keyChanges: New MQB A0 platform debut for Ibiza V(KJ1 chassis)
EA211 1.0 TSI(DKRF/DKLA) replaces EA111 1.2 TSI as mainstream petrol
Carry-over CAYC 1.6 TDI for diesel; no new diesel codes at launch
Reference, SE, Style, XCELLENCE, FR trim structure retained
knownFor: DKRF 1.0 TSI shows improved refinement but direct injection requires intake maintenance
No timing chain crisis on EA211; replacement demand initially lower than Ibiza IV
CAYC diesel carry-over means familiar injector/EGR patterns persist for diesel buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017 Seat Ibiza replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Ibiza 2017 engine code • DKRF vs CBZB reliability • 1.0 TSI intake cleaning cost

years.description: Engine line-up stabilises with DKRF/DKLA 1.0 TSI dominating; first DADA 1.5 TSI Evo units appear in FR models.
keyChanges: Introduction of DADA 1.5 TSI Evo(150 PS) for FR facelift models
Minor calibration updates to DKRF/DKLA; no major reliability changes
Growing awareness of EA211 intake carbon maintenance needs
mainEngines: DADA- 1.5L petrol TSI Evo(150 PS)(FR late 2018+)
knownFor: DADA 1.5 TSI Evo introduces cylinder deactivation; early units generally reliable
DKRF/DKLA intake carbon buildup becomes documented maintenance item around 60k miles
Replacement market for Ibiza V still developing; focus remains on Ibiza IV CBZB/CAYC
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2019 Seat Ibiza replacement engines

years.description: Final evolution of Ibiza V sees refined EA211 engines, updated trim structure and growing replacement options for early DKRF units now reaching higher mileage.
keyChanges: Minor styling and tech updates for late KJ1 production
No new engine codes; DKRF/DKLA/DADA continue with calibration refinements
First DKRF units from 2017 now reaching 80k+ miles; replacement enquiries begin to rise
knownFor: DKRF/DKLA replacement market begins to develop as early units age
Intake carbon cleaning remains key maintenance item for direct-injection EA211
DADA 1.5 TSI Evo still under warranty for most UK examples; minimal replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2024 Seat Ibiza replacement engines
years.ticker: Seat Ibiza 2020 engine replacement • DKRF intake cleaning vs rebuild • 1.5 TSI Evo warranty coverage

faq.h2: Seat Ibiza Engine Replacement- Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Seat Ibiza?
items.answer: Yes, if your Ibiza is in good condition otherwise. A rebuilt engine typically costs £750-£2,400 depending on code, often less than the car's value for FR/Cupra or well-maintained SE/XCELLENCE models.
keyPoints: Rebuilt CBZB 1.2 TSI from £750; CAYC 1.6 TDI from £850
FR and Cupra models justify higher replacement investment
Supply& fit available nationwide from £250 additional labour

items.question: What is the most common Seat Ibiza engine problem?
items.answer: The CBZB/CBZC 1.2 TSI timing chain tensioner failure is the single biggest issue, affecting Ibiza IV models from 2009-2017 and often requiring full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs between 60,000-90,000 miles
Rattling noise on cold start is the key warning sign
Rebuilt engines with upgraded tensioners prevent recurrence

items.question: How much does a Seat Ibiza replacement engine cost?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £750 for a 1.2 TSI to £2,800 for a 1.5 TSI FR unit, with used options available from £400 depending on mileage and warranty.
keyPoints: CBZB 1.2 TSI rebuilt: £750-£1,600 supply only
CAYC 1.6 TDI rebuilt: £850-£2,100 supply only
DKRF 1.0 TSI rebuilt: £950-£2,200 supply only

items.question: What engines fit the Seat Ibiza?
items.answer: Only engines with matching engine codes, mountings and ECU compatibility should be fitted- use your registration to verify the exact code for your Ibiza variant and year.
keyPoints: Ibiza IV(6J/6P) uses EA111 codes: CBZB, CAYC, CZEA
Ibiza V(KJ1) uses EA211 codes: DKRF, DKLA, DADA
Cross-generation swaps require specialist modification and are not recommended

items.question: Does a rebuilt Seat Ibiza engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes- every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with some suppliers offering extended terms.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering

items.question: How much does a timing chain replacement cost on a Seat Ibiza 1.2 TSI?
items.answer: A preventative timing chain kit costs £450-£750 fitted by a specialist, but if the chain has already jumped or damaged valves, a rebuilt CBZB engine(£750-£1,600) is often better value.
keyPoints: Preventative chain kit: £450-£750 fitted if caught early
Valve repair after failure: £1,100-£1,800 often not cost-effective
Rebuilt CBZB with upgraded tensioner: £750-£1,600 with warranty

items.question: What is my Seat Ibiza engine code and where do I find it?
items.answer: Your engine code is stamped on the engine block(usually near the gearbox flange) and listed on your V5C logbook under "P.5"- common codes include CBZB, CAYC, DKRF.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook section P.5 for factory engine code
Physical stamp location: engine block near gearbox or on timing cover
Enter your registration online for instant code verification via DVLA

items.question: Can I get a Seat Ibiza engine fitted near me?
items.answer: Yes- EngineMarket's network includes specialists offering supply& fit services nationwide, with typical fitting costs of £250-£650 depending on engine accessibility and workshop location.
keyPoints: Supply& fit available from vetted UK Seat Ibiza specialists
Typical fitting cost: £250-£650 added to engine price
Many suppliers offer collection/delivery and courtesy car options

items.question: Are used Seat Ibiza engines reliable?
items.answer: Used engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, non-accident donors and compression-tested, but carry shorter warranties(3-6 months) versus rebuilt units(12+ months).
keyPoints: Used CBZB/CAYC from £400-£1,200 depending on mileage
Always request compression test report and mileage verification
Rebuilt engines offer longer warranty and upgraded failure-point components

items.question: What's the cheapest way to replace a Seat Ibiza engine?
items.answer: A used engine from a reputable breaker is the lowest upfront cost(£400-£900), but a rebuilt unit with warranty often provides better long-term value and peace of mind.
keyPoints: Used CBZB/CAYC: £400-£900 supply only, 3-6 month warranty typical
Rebuilt CBZB/CAYC: £750-£2,100 supply only, 12-month warranty standard
Factor in fitting costs(£250-£650) and potential additional repairs

headingLines: Seat Ibiza Engine Replacement- Frequently Asked Questions
